title: cassia tetraphylla var. brevipes
intro: Cassia tetraphylla var. brevipes, commonly known as four-angled cassia, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antibacterial properties. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce stress, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes, among others. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
